analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 12 finding(s) from 13 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug bus bypasses any master authentication, allowing unrestricted access to APB peripherals.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / APB peripheral registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: lint_debug port connected to DBG_RX in soc_interconnect.
demux routes DBG_RX to PER region, APB bridge target.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/Debug master via s_lint_jtag_bus or s_lint_debug_bus Operation: read/write Object: APB peripheral registers Expected guard: Debug authentication lock before granting APB access Observed behavior: s_lint_debug_bus connected to soc_interconnect_wrap, demuxed to PER region that includes APB bus via periph_bus_wrap. No authentication gating found. s_lint_jtag_bus not connected to this path. Path: s_lint_debug_bus -> soc_interconnect_wrap -> soc_interconnect (DBG_RX) -> demux to PER -> periph_bus_wrap -> APB node -> APB peripherals Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug master can read/write SoC control, FLL, GPIO, clock divider, and other security-sensitive APB registers without authentication.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: s_lint_jtag_bus not observed on path; only lang_debug confirms unauthenticated access.

findings.summary: JTAG control register unprotected, writable by any APB master.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / soc_jtag_reg_o / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 331-334: r_jtag_rego <= PWDATA[JTAG_REG_SIZE-1:0]; no lock or privilege signal used.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Any APB master writing via s_soc_ctrl_bus Operation: write Object: soc_jtag_reg_o Expected guard: Write restricted to trusted masters or lock bit Observed behavior: apb_soc_ctrl register write logic for REG_JTAGREG (offset 0x74) updates r_jtag_rego directly on APB write with PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. No privilege check, no lock bit used. Path: APB write -> apb_soc_ctrl (PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE) -> r_jtag_rego assignment Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any APB master can modify JTAG control register, potentially altering JTAG operation.

findings.summary: Critical boot address registers writable without any lock or lifecycle protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_boot_addr_o / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 187-190: r_bootaddr <= PWDATA; Lines 354-357: r_cluster_boot <= PWDATA; no lock logic.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB writer via s_soc_ctrl_bus Operation: write Object: fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_boot_addr_o Expected guard: One-time lock or write disable after boot Observed behavior: r_bootaddr (fc_bootaddr_o) writable via REG_FCBOOT (0x04) with no lock. r_cluster_boot (cluster_boot_addr_o) writable via REG_CLUSTER_BOOT_ADDR0/1 (0xC8/CC) with no lock. Reset default: fc_bootaddr=0x1A000000. Path: APB write -> apb_soc_ctrl -> r_bootaddr / r_cluster_boot Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Boot address can be changed at any time, redirecting boot flow.

findings.summary: Cluster power/reset control lacks any write protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 339-345: write to cluster control bits without lock.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master via s_soc_ctrl_bus Operation: write Object: cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o Expected guard: Lock bit or secure-mode-only access Observed behavior: REG_CLUSTER_CTRL (0x70) directly updates r_cluster_byp, r_cluster_pow, r_cluster_fetch_enable, r_cluster_rstn. No lock used. Path: APB write -> apb_soc_ctrl -> r_cluster_* registers Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any APB master can power down, bypass isolation, or reset the cluster.

findings.summary: FLL configuration unprotected, accessible by any APB master.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / FLL configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 237-271: write access without lock; no use of dft_cg_enable_i.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master or DFT logic Operation: write Object: FLL configuration registers Expected guard: Write lock after setup or secure-only write Observed behavior: apb_fll_if provides direct APB access to FLL1/2/3 address spaces when PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. No lock mechanism; write_ready depends on FSM, dft_cg_enable_i not used in this module. Path: APB write -> apb_fll_if -> fllx_req, fllx_data, fllx_add Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: FLL settings can be changed by any APB master, potentially disrupting clocks.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No evidence of dft_cg_enable_i effect; likely benign.

findings.summary: Potential lack of lock or access control; requires further inspection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / gpio_dir, gpio_out registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Defines REG_GPIOLOCK (5'b10010); write logic not inspected.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master via s_gpio_bus Operation: write Object: gpio_dir, gpio_out registers Expected guard: Privilege-based write protection or lock bit Observed behavior: apb_gpio module defines a GPIOLOCK register (offset 0x48), suggesting a lock mechanism may exist. Full write logic not observed in tool outputs. Path: Not fully confirmed. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If lock missing or ineffective, GPIO control could be abused.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full write enable logic and lock behavior not observed; need to see if GPIOLOCK prevents direction/output changes.

findings.summary: Insufficient evidence to assess write protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / apb_adv_timer configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Port list with dft_cg_enable_i, but no internal logic.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master via s_adv_timer_bus Operation: write Object: apb_adv_timer configuration registers Expected guard: Privilege-based access or lock bits Observed behavior: Only port declarations observed; no internal write logic or lock bits visible. Path: Unknown. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal register write conditions and any lock registers not inspected.

findings.summary: Clock divider registers permanently writable without a lock.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / clk_div0, clk_div1, clk_div2 registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 62-83: direct assignment on APB write, no lock.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master via clock bus Operation: write Object: clk_div0, clk_div1, clk_div2 registers Expected guard: One-time lock after configuration Observed behavior: apb_clkdiv write logic updates r_clkdiv0/1/2 directly when PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. No lock bit; writes always enabled. Path: APB write -> apb_clkdiv -> r_clkdiv* registers Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Clock dividers can be changed at any time, potentially crashing system or affecting security peripherals.

findings.summary: No clear violation of isolation found; address mapping must be checked separately.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / APB slaves / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Lines 62-65: psel_o[i] = (paddr_i >= START_ADDR_i[i]) && (paddr_i <= END_ADDR_i[i]); no PSLVERR generation.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB master driving node Operation: read/write Object: APB slaves Expected guard: Correct address mapping, no gaps, error response on unmapped addresses Observed behavior: apb_node generates psel based on start/end address comparisons. No logic for PSLVERR when no slave selected; bus may hang. Address mapping constants not inspected. Path: apb_node address decode Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Access to unmapped addresses may cause bus stall; no security isolation bypass observed for debug.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Address mapping values (periph_bus_defines.sv) not available to check overlaps/gaps. PSLVERR behavior on unmapped addresses needs verification.

findings.summary: Cannot confirm DMA access control without inspecting udma_subsystem and interconnect firewall.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / System memory/peripherals / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Ports for udma_subsystem listed; no detail on internal address checks.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA engine (udma_subsystem) Operation: read/write Object: System memory/peripherals Expected guard: Address-range filtering or master-ID based access control Observed behavior: udma_subsystem module not inspected; only its bus ports visible in pulp_soc.sv. No MPU/PMP observed. Path: Unknown. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential unrestricted DMA access to secure regions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: udma_subsystem.sv contents not available; address protection logic unknown.

findings.summary: No protection layer between main interconnect and APB peripherals, enabling unauthorised access.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / APB peripherals behind periph_bus_wrap / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Full file shows only address mapping and apb_node_wrap instantiation; no firewall.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Bus master on main interconnect Operation: read/write Object: APB peripherals behind periph_bus_wrap Expected guard: Security attribute check or firewall Observed behavior: periph_bus_wrap is a simple wrapper instantiating apb_node_wrap; no access-control signals processed. It directly bridges APB slave to master channels without filtering. Path: Main bus -> periph_bus_wrap -> apb_node_wrap -> APB peripherals Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Masters on main bus (including debug) can directly access APB peripherals without additional authorization.

findings.summary: No evidence of security bypass via DFT test signal, but further analysis required.
findings.vulnerability_category: override / Clock gating or protection mechanisms / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: Port dft_cg_enable_i present; internal usage unknown.
Port dft_cg_enable_i present; internal usage unknown.

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Test interface driving dft_cg_enable_i Operation: override Object: Clock gating or protection mechanisms Expected guard: Benign effect or secure activation Observed behavior: dft_cg_enable_i is a port on apb_gpio and apb_adv_timer, but its effect on register writes or lock bits not shown. No internal usage observed in apb_fll_if or apb_soc_ctrl. Path: dft_cg_enable_i -> peripheral logic (unknown) Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Could potentially bypass clock gating, but impact on security-critical protections unclear.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: RTL for dft_cg_enable_i effect in GPIO and timer not inspected.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
